title: 10 Herbal Teas to Provide Immediate Relief from Burning Feet
intro_desc: Herbal teas for burning feet are a natural remedy that helps alleviate the discomfort and pain associated with burning sensations in the feet. These teas are made from various herbs that possess anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which help to calm the skin and reduce irritation. The best herbal teas for burning feet include Urtica dioica, Calendula officinalis, Aloe vera, Aloe barbadensis, Equisetum arvense, Arnica montana, Hypericum perforatum, Zingiber officinale, Glycyrrhiza glabra, and Achillea millefolium. If left untreated, burning feet can lead to anxiety, depression, and disrupt daily activities, affecting one's quality of life and overall well-being.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra tea is a good option for people with burning feet due to its anti-inflammatory properties. This means it can help reduce swelling and pain in the feet. Glycyrrhizin, a compound found in the tea, has the ability to block the production of certain chemicals in the body that cause pain and inflammation. Additionally, Glycyrrhiza glabra tea has been shown to improve circulation, which can help increase blood flow to the feet and promote healing. This can be especially helpful for people with conditions like peripheral artery disease, which causes poor circulation. Drinking Glycyrrhiza glabra tea may also help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing itchiness and discomfort associated with burning feet. Some people also report that it can help to reduce the sensation of heat and burning in the feet.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Saponins are good for burning feet because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and cool the skin, reducing irritation and discomfort caused by heat.

Glycyrrhizin is not found, it is actually found in Glycyrrhiza glabra (licorice root) but some sources list Glycyrrhizin as being good for burning feet, as it has anti-inflammatory properties that may help to reduce swelling and discomfort in the feet, and its ability to soothe and calm irritated skin may provide relief from burning sensations.
Flavonoids are good for burning feet because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and ease the pain caused by burning sensations.
Polyphenols are good for burning feet because they have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and soothe the skin, providing relief from discomfort and pain.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Melissa officinalis herbal tea that helps with burning feet, start by gathering dried Melissa officinalis leaves, which is also known as lemon balm. Begin by boiling one cup of water in a kettle. Next, add a teaspoon of dried Melissa officinalis leaves to the boiling water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes. After the steeping time is up, strain the tea into a cup using a tea strainer or a piece of cheesecloth to remove the leaves. You can then add a bit of honey or lemon to taste, although it's best to drink it plain. Drink the tea once or twice a day to help soothe and cool burning sensations in the feet. You can also add a few drops of peppermint essential oil to the tea for extra relief from burning sensations. Remember to consult a doctor before consuming any herbal remedies, especially if you have any underlying health conditions.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis tea can be good for burning feet because of its natural cooling and soothing properties. The tea is made from the leaves of the aloe plant, which has been used for centuries to treat skin irritations and burns. The aloe plant contains a gel-like substance that has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and pain in the feet. When you soak your feet in aloe barbadensis tea, the antioxidants and vitamins in the tea can help to calm and cool the skin, reducing the burning sensation. The tea can also help to lock in moisture, preventing dryness and cracking of the skin. By drinking aloe barbadensis tea, you can also help to nourish your skin from the inside out, promoting healthy skin and preventing future irritation. The tea is also known to improve circulation, which can help to reduce pain and discomfort in the feet.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Melissa officinalis herbal tea that helps with burning feet, you will need a few ingredients and some basic equipment. First, gather a handful of Melissa officinalis leaves, which is also known as lemon balm, and dry them completely to prevent spoilage. Next, boil a pot of water and add a tablespoon of the dried leaves to it. Allow the mixture to simmer for 5-7 minutes, or until the leaves have released their flavor and aroma. Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or a piece of cheesecloth to remove the leaves. Then, add a tablespoon of honey or sugar to sweeten the tea, if desired. Finally, drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help soothe and calm burning feet. Note that it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using Melissa officinalis herbal tea for medicinal purposes, especially if you have any underlying health conditions or allergies.

By drinking aloe vera tea regularly, you can keep your skin hydrated and reduce the risk of irritation and burning. This can be especially helpful if you're someone who spends a lot of time on their feet, such as a nurse or a server. Drinking aloe vera tea may also help to prevent blisters and other foot problems.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Melissa officinalis herbal tea that helps with burning feet, start by gathering the necessary ingredients. Melissa officinalis, also known as lemon balm, is a natural herb that soothes and cools the skin. You'll need a handful of dried lemon balm leaves, a teapot, and a cup. Begin by boiling water in the teapot and then add a teaspoon of dried lemon balm leaves to the water. Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes, or until the liquid has turned a light green color and the aroma of lemon balm fills the air. Next, strain the liquid into a cup to remove the herb leaves and then let the tea cool down to a comfortable drinking temperature. Once it's cooled, you can enjoy the tea as is or add a bit of honey to sweeten it. Drinking Melissa officinalis herbal tea can help to soothe and calm burning feet, while also providing relief from other minor skin irritations.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum tea, also known as St. John's Wort tea, is a herbal remedy that has been used to help soothe burning feet for centuries. The active compounds in this tea, called hyperforins, have anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and ease pain in the feet. When the skin on your feet is irritated, it can cause redness, itching, and burning sensations. The hyperforins in St. John's Wort tea can help to calm down this irritation, reducing the burning sensation and promoting healing. The tea's antiseptic properties can also help to prevent infections that can cause foot problems, such as fungal infections. Additionally, the tea has antioxidant properties that can help to protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to inflammation and irritation.

Strain the tea into a cup using a tea strainer or a piece of cheesecloth to remove the leaves. Let the tea cool down for a few minutes before drinking it. As for drinking it to help with burning feet, try making a tea with this herb once or twice a day, and apply a cooling compress or cold water to your feet to help ease the burning sensation.

remedies.remedy_desc: Urtica dioica tea, also known as nettle tea, is a natural remedy for burning feet. The roots and leaves of the nettle plant contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and calm the skin. When you drink nettle tea, these compounds are absorbed into your body and can help to reduce swelling and pain in your feet. This is especially helpful if you have conditions like plantar fasciitis or athlete's foot, which can cause burning and itching sensations. The tea also has antioxidant properties, which can help to protect your skin from damage caused by free radicals. Additionally, nettle tea is rich in vitamins and minerals, including vitamin C and iron, which are essential for healthy skin and can help to promote healing. Drinking nettle tea may also help to reduce inflammation and ease pain in your feet, making it a popular natural remedy for people who experience burning feet.

First, boil some water in a pot and remove it from the heat. Then, add 1-2 teaspoons of dried Melissa leaves to a tea infuser or a small piece of cheesecloth. To help soothe burning feet, try soaking your feet in warm water with 1-2 cups of the Melissa tea for 10-15 minutes. remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Melissa officinalis herbal tea that helps with burning feet, start by gathering a handful of fresh Melissa officinalis leaves or 1 teaspoon of dried leaves. If using fresh leaves, gently wash them with cold water and pat them dry with a clean cloth. Next, place the leaves in a teapot or a heat-resistant cup, and pour in 1 cup of boiling water. Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes to allow the herbal properties to release. Strain the tea into another cup to remove the leaves, and add a sweetener like honey if desired. For burning feet, Melissa officinalis tea is best taken after a warm bath or shower, and ideally before bedtime to help your feet recover and relax during the night.

Ginger also helps to improve blood flow, which means your feet get more oxygen and nutrients. This can help to speed up the healing process and make your feet feel better faster. For dried leaves, simply measure out the desired amount, usually 1 to 2 teaspoons per cup of boiling water. After the steeping time, strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the leaves. You can also add honey or lemon to taste, if desired. Finally, drink the tea when it's still warm, ideally before bedtime, as it may help to soothe and calm burning feet while promoting a restful night's sleep.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa tea, also known as turmeric tea, is a natural remedy that may help with burning feet. Additionally, turmeric tea has antioxidant properties that help fight off free radicals, which can cause further damage and pain. Drinking turmeric tea may also help improve blood circulation to the feet, which can aid in the healing process and reduce discomfort. supplementary_best: To remedy burning feet, a combination of herbal teas from 'Zingiber officinale', 'Curcuma longa', and 'Glycyrrhiza glabra' can be effective. Zingiber officinale, or ginger, has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and ease pain in the feet. Curcuma longa, or turmeric, contains curcumin, a compound that reduces inflammation and has antioxidant properties, which can help soothe burning sensations. Glycyrrhiza glabra, or licorice root, has antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties that can help calm irritated skin and reduce inflammation. This combination is the best because it addresses the root causes of burning feet, including inflammation and irritation. Drinking a warm tea made from equal parts of these three herbs can help to calm and soothe burning feet.
supplementary_avoid: If you have burning feet, there are some herbal teas you should avoid.  One of these is tea made from 'Capsicum annuum', also known as cayenne pepper. Capsicum contains a chemical called capsaicin, which can increase the burning sensation in your feet. Drinking cayenne pepper tea may make your burning feet feel worse.  Another herbal tea to avoid is 'Ephedra sinica'. Ephedra contains a chemical called ephedrine, which can cause your blood vessels to constrict. This can reduce blood flow to your feet, making the burning sensation worse.  You should also avoid 'Valeriana officinalis' tea, also known as valerian root tea. Valerian root can cause your blood vessels to constrict, which can reduce blood flow to your feet and make burning feet feel worse.  Additionally, tea made from 'Angelica archangelica' should be avoided. Angelica can cause your blood vessels to constrict, which can reduce blood flow to your feet and make burning feet feel worse.  Lastly, 'Sassafras albidum' tea should be avoided. Sassafras contains a chemical called safrole, which can cause nerve damage and make burning feet feel worse.  These herbal teas may make your burning feet feel worse, so it's best to avoid them.
supplementary_other_remedies: Epsom salt baths can help soothe burning feet. Epsom salt contains magnesium, a mineral that helps relax muscles and reduce inflammation. When added to warm water, it can draw out toxins and ease discomfort in the feet. The magnesium also helps to calm the nervous system, which can help reduce burning sensations.  Cold compresses or ice packs can also provide relief from burning feet. Applying a cold compress or an ice pack to the affected area can help numb the pain and reduce inflammation. This can give temporary relief from burning sensations and help reduce swelling.  Another remedy is to wear socks made from natural fibers like cotton or wool. These materials can help keep feet dry and prevent moisture buildup, which can contribute to burning sensations. Wearing socks made from synthetic materials can trap moisture, leading to discomfort and burning.  Exfoliating the feet, particularly the heels and soles, can also help alleviate burning sensations. Removing dead skin cells can help improve blood circulation and reduce pressure on the skin, which can help reduce burning sensations.  Olive oil or coconut oil massages can also provide relief from burning feet. Massaging these oils into the feet can help moisturize and soften the skin, reducing dryness and discomfort. This can also help improve blood circulation and reduce pressure on the skin, which can help alleviate burning sensations.
supplementary_related_ailments: People who experience burning feet may also have other health issues that can be treated with medicinal herbs. One common problem is nerve damage, which can cause numbness, tingling, and pain in the feet. A herbal remedy that may help is turmeric, which contains a compound called curcumin that has anti-inflammatory properties and can reduce nerve pain. Another issue is high blood sugar levels, which can lead to numbness and pain in the feet. A medicinal herb that can help regulate blood sugar levels is berberine, which has been shown to lower blood sugar levels and improve insulin sensitivity. Burning feet can also be a symptom of poor circulation, which can lead to cold hands and feet. A herbal remedy that may improve circulation is ginkgo biloba, which can help dilate blood vessels and improve blood flow. Additionally, people with burning feet may also experience vitamin deficiencies, such as vitamin B12 deficiency, which can cause numbness and tingling in the feet. A medicinal herb that can help treat vitamin deficiencies is ashwagandha, which can help increase vitamin levels and reduce oxidative stress.
